lock_policy: Dated official pick files are append-only. Re-running the engine for the same date reuses the existing dated file instead of changing official picks.
note: Official picks require market edge, model probability >= 72%, Lab Rating >= 8.0/10, pitcher support, and no major bullpen caution.

labScoreBreakdown.note: Lab Rating grades setup quality. It is not win probability.

pick_team.label: Normal
pick_team.workload_read: Manageable recent workload. Bullpen should still be part of the full-game read.
pick_team.score_reason: Normal (5.0/10): 10 recent relief innings, recency-weighted (pitched yesterday). Recent innings count more; rest days lower the score. Workload only — see efficiency for how well the pen has pitched.
pick_team.formula: 45 + (recency-weighted (BP IP - 3/game) x 4.2) + (recency-weighted back-to-back arms x 8). Each game decays by half every 2 days, so rest days lower the score.

context_only.note: Reliever counts are displayed for context only and do not add score points.

pick_team.efficiency_reason: Dominant (8.3/10): 0.9 ERA and 0.4 WHIP over 10 relief innings (1 ER, 3 H, 1 BB) in the last three days.
pick_team.efficiency_formula: 50 - clamp((ERA - 4.20) x 6, -25, 25) x confidence - clamp((WHIP - 1.30) x 15, -20, 20) x confidence, confidence = clamp(BP IP / 6, 0.35, 1)

opponent.label: High risk
opponent.workload_read: Heavy recent workload. Late-game pitching condition could materially affect the read.
opponent.score_reason: High risk (9.0/10): 17.7 recent relief innings, recency-weighted — 9 in the last game (pitched yesterday), 2 arms worked back-to-back days. Recent innings count more; rest days lower the score. Workload only — see efficiency for how well the pen has pitched.
opponent.formula: 45 + (recency-weighted (BP IP - 3/game) x 4.2) + (recency-weighted back-to-back arms x 8). Each game decays by half every 2 days, so rest days lower the score.

context_only.note: Reliever counts are displayed for context only and do not add score points.

opponent.efficiency_reason: Below average (3.7/10): 5.09 ERA and 1.81 WHIP over 17.7 relief innings (10 ER, 26 H, 6 BB) in the last three days.
opponent.efficiency_formula: 50 - clamp((ERA - 4.20) x 6, -25, 25) x confidence - clamp((WHIP - 1.30) x 15, -20, 20) x confidence, confidence = clamp(BP IP / 6, 0.35, 1)

moneyline.why: Tampa Bay Rays is an official moneyline pick because it clears both gates: 74.3% model win probability and 9.4/10 Lab Rating. LyDia projects 74.3% against a 49.4% no-vig market number, a 24.9% model edge at -102. Tampa Bay Rays owns the starting pitcher edge by 19 points. Bullpen read: Supports LyDia side. Tampa Bay Rays's pen efficiency: Dominant (8.3/10). Lineup check: Tampa Bay Rays is swinging a hot bat (+0.057 OPS); Toronto Blue Jays is in a cold stretch (-0.055 OPS). Recent form supports this side and is included in the unified run projection.
moneyline.risk: No model can see every live lineup, injury, or late bullpen availability update. Recheck official news before first pitch.

moneyline.why: Atlanta Braves is an official moneyline pick because it clears both gates: 77.5% model win probability and 8.5/10 Lab Rating. LyDia projects 77.5% against a 69.4% no-vig market number, a 8.0% model edge at -248. Atlanta Braves owns the starting pitcher edge by 46 points. Bullpen read: Both bullpens stressed. Atlanta Braves's pen efficiency: Below average (3.4/10). Lineup check: Atlanta Braves is swinging a hot bat (+0.054 OPS); San Diego Padres is swinging a hot bat (+0.156 OPS). Recent form leans against this side and is included in the unified run projection.
moneyline.risk: Primary caution: both bullpens show elevated recent workload. Recheck official news before first pitch.
